claims.claim: Conventional curriculum design often defaults to exhaustive, encyclopedia-style topic coverage, which evidence suggests contributes to curriculum overload and shallow learning outcomes.

claims.claim: Under the interpretation that "anti-encyclopedia" means anti-exhaustive-coverage and anti-passive-reference-traversal, the AEC achieves a design viability score of 27/28, exceeding the pre-specified minimum viable threshold of 21.

claims.claim: The "Anti-Encyclopedia Curriculum" (AEC) concept responds to this tension by proposing that curriculum should not be organized as an exhaustive reference traversal, but rather around generative problems, prerequisite knowledge structures, and evidence-backed learning mechanisms.
